definition: Pronunciation spelling of the, representing dialectal English.
To press lightly in a repetitive motion with a soft object without rubbing.
To make slightly wet or soiled by spattering or sprinkling a liquid (such as water, mud, or paint) on it; to bedabble.
The section of a pedestal above the base.
A bulbous plant of the genus Narcissus, with yellow flowers and a trumpet shaped corona, especially Narcissus pseudonarcissus, the national flower of Wales.
A stabbing weapon, similar to a sword but with a short, double-edged blade.
That occurs or attends every day, or at least every working day.
(also dairy products or dairy produce) Products produced from milk.
A wild flowering plant of species Bellis perennis of the family Asteraceae, with a yellow head and white petals
Any of many dried husked pulses (legume), including peas, beans and lentils.
To waste time in trivial activities, or in idleness; to trifle.

A structure placed across a flowing body of water to stop the flow or part of the flow, generally for purposes such as retaining or diverting some of the water or retarding the release of accumulated water to avoid abrupt flooding.
Injury or harm; the condition or measure of something not being intact.
Usually capitalized as Dame: a title equivalent to Sir for a female knight.
In a state between dry and wet; moderately wet; moist.
A title of honour or respect similar to "master" or "Sir", used of historical and legendary figures of the past.
A sequence of rhythmic steps or movements usually performed to music, for pleasure or as a form of social interaction.
A person who dances, usually as a hobby, an occupation, or a profession.
The activity of taking part in a dance.
Any of the several species of plant in the genus Taraxacum, characterised by yellow flower heads and notched, broad-ended leaves, especially the common dandelion (Taraxacum officinale).
Exposure to likely harm; risk of death or serious injury.
To hang loosely with the ability to swing.
A Puerto Rican music genre associated with ballroom dancing.
A fish found in the Severn River; a dart or dace.
To have enough courage (to do something).
A male given name transferred from the surname, variant of Darren.
present participle and gerund of dare
Having an absolute or (more often) relative lack of light.
To make dark or darker by reducing light.
With insufficient light for easy discernment or comprehension.
The state of being dark; lack of light; the absolute or comparative absence of light.
Often used as an affectionate term of address: a person who is very dear to one.
A pointed missile weapon, intended to be thrown by the hand; for example, a short lance or javelin.
Any of the following symbols: ‒ (figure dash), – (en dash), — (em dash), or ― (horizontal bar).
A collection of (usually) organized information in a regular structure, usually but not necessarily in a machine-readable format accessible by a computer.
The fruit of the date palm, Phoenix dactylifera, somewhat in the shape of an olive, containing a soft, sweet pulp and enclosing a hard kernel.
Something known or assumed as fact, and is made the basis of reasoning or inference which an intellectual system of any sort (such as knowledge or theoretical framework) is constructed.
Excrement or clay used as a bonding material in construction.
A developmental stage of certain nematode larvae in which they exhibit increased durability.

The eldest son of the king of France. Under the Valois and Bourbon dynasties, the Dauphin of France, generally shortened to Dauphin, was heir apparent to the throne of France. The title derived from the main title of the Dauphin, Dauphin of Viennois.
To begin to brighten with daylight.
The time when the Sun is above the horizon and it lights the sky.
The beginning of the day; the first moment of daylight.
The natural light that is ambient in daytime, being mostly sunlight (both direct and indirect, on either sunny days or cloudy days).
The time of daylight; the time between sunrise and sunset.
To confuse or overpower the sight of (someone or something, such as a sensor) by means of excessive brightness.
The name of the Cyrillic script letter Д / д.
No longer living; deceased. (Also used as a noun.)
A time limit in the form of a date on or before which something must be completed.
To make deaf, either temporarily or permanently.
Loud enough to cause temporary or permanent hearing loss.
A division, a portion, a share, a part, a piece.
One who deals in goods, especially automobiles; a middleman.

A senior official in a college or university, who may be in charge of a division or faculty (for example, the dean of science) or have some other advisory or disciplinary function (for example, the dean of students).
In a dear or precious manner.
A period or condition when food is rare and hence expensive; famine.
The cessation of life and all associated processes; the end of an organism's existence as an entity independent from its environment and its return to an inert, nonliving state.
The bed on which someone dies.
An event or enterprise that ends suddenly and disastrously, often with humiliating consequences.
To lower in character, quality, or value; to degrade.
Open to debate; not fully proved or confirmed.
To participate in a debate; to dispute, argue, especially in a public arena.
A tin container used for kerosene etc.
In bookkeeping, an entry in the left hand column of an account.
Rubble, wreckage, scattered remains of something destroyed.
An action, state of mind, or object one has an obligation to perform for another, adopt toward another, or give to another.
A person or firm that owes money; one in debt; one who owes a debt.
A performer's first performance to the public, in sport, the arts or some other area.
A group, set, or series of ten , particularly
A state of moral or artistic decline or deterioration; decay
Rot; any processes or result of organic matter being gradually decomposed, especially by microbial action.
An act or practice intended to deceive; a trick.
The twelfth and last month of the Gregorian calendar, following November and preceding the January of the following year, containing the southern solstice.
The quality of being decent; propriety.
To cause to change from being concentrated at one point or site to being distributed across several.
An instance of actions and/or schemes fabricated to mislead someone into believing a lie or inaccuracy.
To resolve (a contest, problem, dispute, etc.); to choose, determine, or settle.
simple past and past participle of decide
In a manner which leaves little question; definitely, clearly.
Concerning numbers expressed in decimal or mathematical calculations performed using decimal.
To kill one-tenth of (a group), (historical, specifically) as a military punishment in the Roman army selected by lot, usually carried out by the surviving soldiers.
To convert a code or cipher to plain text.
Having the power or quality of deciding a question or controversy; putting an end to contest or controversy; final; conclusive.
Any raised flat surface that can be walked on: a balcony; a porch; a raised patio; a flat rooftop.
An emphatic or formal act of saying, telling or asserting something, by speech or writing; a decisive assertion or proclamation.
simple past and past participle of declare
The style of decoration of a room or building.
The act of adorning, embellishing, or honoring; ornamentation.
A person or object meant to lure somebody into danger.
Of a quantity, to become smaller.
Weakened or worn out from age or wear.
A barangay of Umingan, Pangasinan, Philippines.
A division, at one end of a tennis court, for spectators.
To set apart for a deity or for religious purposes; consecrate.
The act of dedicating or the state of being dedicated.
A traditional short Spanish unit of length, usually about equal to 1.75 cm.
To reach (a conclusion) by applying rules of logic or other forms of reasoning to given premises or known facts.
To take one thing from another; remove from; make smaller or less by some amount.
That which is deducted; that which is subtracted or removed.
An action or act; something that is done.
To hold in belief or estimation; to adjudge as a conclusion; to regard as being; to evaluate according to one's beliefs; to account.
Extending, reaching or positioned far from a point of reference, especially downwards.
To a deep extent or degree; very greatly.
A ruminant mammal with hooves and often antlers, of the family Cervidae, or one of several similar animals from related families of the order Artiodactyla, such as the musk deer or mouse deer.
The act of injuring another person's reputation by any slanderous communication, written or oral; the wrong of maliciously injuring the good name of another.
The condition of failing to meet an obligation.
To overcome in battle or contest.
An act or incidence of defecting.
To ward off attacks against; to fight to protect; to guard.
Serving, or suitable, for defense; defensive, defending.
Someone who defends people or property.
The action of defending or protecting from attack, danger, or injury.
Lacking any form of defense; vulnerable; open to attack.
The feeling, or spirit of being defiant.
Lacking something essential; often construed with in.
Deficiency in amount or quality; a falling short; lack.
To determine with precision; to mark out with distinctness; to ascertain or exhibit clearly.
A statement of the meaning of a word, word group, sign, or symbol; especially, a dictionary definition.
To remove air or some other gas from within an elastic container, e.g. a balloon or tyre.
To make (something) deviate from its original path or position.
The process of destroying a forest and replacing it with something else, especially with an agricultural system.
Having an unusual and unattractive shape; deformed, misshapen; hence, hideous, ugly.
Quick and neat in action; skillful.
No longer in use or active, nor expected to be again.
To challenge (someone) or brave (a hazard or opposition).
Having deteriorated, degraded or fallen from normal, coherent, balanced and desirable to undesirable and typically abnormal.
The act of reducing in rank, character, or reputation, or of abasing; a lowering from one's standing or rank in office or society
To lower in value or social position.

A stage of proficiency or qualification in a course of study, now especially an award bestowed by a university/college, as a certification of academic achievement. (In the United States, can include secondary schools.)
All sorts of outworks in general, at a distance from the main works; any advanced works for protection or cover.
Alternative spelling of dey (“informal term of address used when trying to get someone's attention.”).
Synonym of divinity: the state, position, or fact of being a god.
The symbol ∇ used to denote the gradient operator.
A period of time before an event occurs; the act of delaying; procrastination; lingering inactivity.
A person authorized to act as representative for another; a deputy.
To remove, get rid of or erase, especially written or printed material, or data on a computer or other device.
Intentionally, or after deliberation; not accidentally.
Easily damaged or requiring careful handling.
Pleasing to the sense of taste; tasty.
Pleasant; pleasing, bringing enjoyment, satisfaction, or pleasure.
Late or failing to pay a debt or other financial obligation, like a mortgage or loan.
Being in the state of delirium.

A temporary mental state with a sudden onset, usually reversible, including symptoms of confusion, inability to concentrate, disorientation, anxiety, and sometimes hallucinations. Causes can include dehydration, drug intoxication, and severe infection.
To set free from restraint or danger.
The act of setting free or extricating from danger, imprisonment, bondage, evil, etc.
third-person singular simple present indicative of deliver
A female given name originating as a coinage.
The fourth letter of the modern Greek alphabet Δ, δ.
To deceive into believing something which is false; to lead into error; to dupe.
To dig into the ground, especially with a shovel.
A demesne, especially the ancient demesne claimed by William the Conqueror.
The desire to purchase goods and services.
The act of marking off a boundary or setting a limit, notably by belligerents signing a treaty or ceasefire.
The social, non-verbal behaviors (such as body language and facial expressions) that are characteristic of a person.

A progressive decline in cognitive function due to damage or disease in the brain beyond what might be expected from normal aging. Areas particularly affected include memory, attention, judgement, language and problem solving.
A quality of being inadequate; a disadvantage, a fault.
The conveyance or transfer of an estate, either in fee for life or for years, most commonly the latter.
Rule by the people, especially as a form of government; either directly or through elected representatives (representative democracy).
A supporter of democracy; an advocate of democratic politics (originally (historical) as opposed to the aristocrats in Revolutionary France).
Pertaining to democracy; constructed upon or in line with the principle of government chosen by the people.
The study of the characteristics of human populations, especially with regards to their makeup and fluctuations and the social causes behind these.
To destroy (buildings, etc.), especially in a planned or intentional fashion.
The process of demolishing or destroying buildings or other structures.

The act of demonstrating; showing or explaining something.
One who demonstrates anything, or proves beyond doubt.
To destroy the morale of; to dishearten.
Chiefly followed by to, and sometimes by at or on: to object or be reluctant; to balk, to take exception.
A small cavern or hollow place in the side of a hill, or among rocks; especially, a cave used by a wild animal for shelter or concealment.

A textile often made of cotton with a distinct diagonal pattern.
The act of naming or designating.
To make known in a formal manner; to proclaim; to announce; to declare.
The amount of a certain phenomenon (such as stuff, things, energy, or otherwise) in a given area (surface area) or volume (amount of space).
A shallow deformation in the surface of an object, produced by an impact.
A medical doctor who specializes in teeth.
A production that is classified somewhere between a demo and an intro.
Any agent acting to eliminate, reduce, mask, or control odor.
The act of departing or something that has departed.

To render a representation of something, using words, sounds, images, or other means.
To reduce the amount of; to remove (a substance from something)
The act of depleting, or the state of being depleted; exhaustion.
To bewail; to weep bitterly over; to feel sorrow for.
To prepare and arrange (originally military unit or units, i.e., to array troops) for use.
The act of deporting or exiling, or the state of being deported; banishment; transportation.
To put down; to lay down; to deposit; to lay aside; to put away.
Sediment or rock that is not native to its present location or is different from the surrounding material. Sometimes refers to ore or gems.
The removal of someone from office.
A storage facility, in particular, a warehouse.

simple past and past participle of depress
A state of mind producing serious, long-term lowering of enjoyment of life or inability to visualize a happy future; any of several mental disorders with this state of mind as a central feature.
The act of depriving, dispossessing, or bereaving.
Used with “of”, to take something away from (someone) and keep it away; to deny someone something.
the vertical distance below a surface; the degree to which something is deep
One appointed as the substitute of others, and empowered to act for them, in their name or their behalf; a substitute in office.
Disdainful indication that something is obvious.
A windstorm characterized by strong, straight-line winds.
Given up by the guardian or owner; abandoned, forsaken.
To laugh at or mock (someone or something) harshly; to ridicule, to scorn.
Expressing or characterized by derision; mocking; ridiculing.
Obtained by derivation; not radical, original, or fundamental.
To obtain or receive (something) from something else.
A person who is skilled in, professes or practices dermatology.
A cross placed at the site of a violent, unexpected death, in memoriam.
Senses relating to moving from a higher to a lower position.
An instance of descending; act of coming down.
A sketch or account of anything in words; a portraiture or representation in language; an enumeration of the essential qualities of a thing or species.
Of, relating to, or providing a description.
A barren area of land or desolate terrain, especially one with little water or vegetation; a wasteland.

To be entitled to, as a result of past actions; to be worthy to have.
Worthy of reward or praise; meritorious.
A specification of an object or process, referring to requirements to be satisfied and thus conditions to be met for them to solve a problem.

A person who designs something, or who designs things as a profession.

Worthy to be desired; pleasing; agreeable.
To want; to wish for earnestly.
Feeling desire; eagerly wishing; eager to obtain.
A table, frame, or case, in past centuries usually with a sloping top but now usually with a flat top, for the use of writers and readers. It often has a drawer or repository underneath.
The top surface of a desk.
To give up as beyond hope or expectation; to despair of.
In dire need (of something); having a dire need or desire.
In a desperate manner; without regard to danger or safety; recklessly.
The act of despairing or becoming desperate; a giving up of hope.
To regard with contempt or scorn.
In spite of, not with standing.
In low spirits from loss of hope or courage.
Of or pertaining to a despot or tyrant.
The last course of a meal, consisting of fruit, sweet confections etc.
The highest voice or part, often a violin or soprano vocal line.
The place set for the end of a journey, or to which something is sent; place or point aimed at.
Confined to a predetermined fate or destiny; certain.
That to which any person or thing is destined; a predetermined state; a condition predestined by the Divine or by human will.
The action of deserting or abandoning.
To damage beyond use or repair; to damage (something) to the point that it effectively ceases to exist.
To take apart from; to take off.
Not physically attached; separated from something to which it could connect.
A part small enough to escape casual notice.
Characterized by attention to detail and thoroughness of treatment.
To keep something from proceeding or coming to completion; to delay.
To discover or find by careful search, examination, or probing.
The act or process of detecting, uncovering, or finding out, the discovery of something new, hidden, or disguised.
A police officer tasked with collecting evidence and information in order to solve a crime; an investigator.
A device capable of registering a specific substance or physical phenomenon, and that optionally sounds an alarm or triggers a warning.
The act of detaining or the state of being detained; hindrance.
Any non-soap cleaning agent, especially a synthetic surfactant.
To make worse; to make inferior in quality or value; to impair.

The act of determining, or the state of being determined.

Decided; resolute, possessing much determination; dogged.

Serving to deter, preventing something from happening.
To dislike (someone or something) intensely; to loathe.
A diversion or deviation from one's original route.
To take away; to withdraw or remove.
Germany (a country in Central Europe, formed in 1949 as West Germany, with its provisional capital Bonn until 1990, when it incorporated East Germany).
The removal or lessening of something's value.
To lower or remove the value of something.
To ruin many or all things over a large area, such as most or all buildings of a city, or cities of a region, or trees of a forest.
To discover, find out; to uncover.
A person or entity engaged in the creation or improvement of certain classes of products.

The process of developing; growth, directed change.
To go off course from; to change course; to change plans.
Any piece of equipment made for a particular purpose, especially a mechanical or electrical one.
Cunning or deceiving, not straightforward or honest, not frank.
To use one’s intellect to plan or design (something).
Duty, business; something that one must do.
To be inherited by someone else; to pass down upon the next person in a succession, especially through failure or loss of an earlier holder.
to give one's time, focus one's efforts, commit oneself, etc. entirely for, on, or to a certain matter; to consecrate.

To eat quickly, greedily, hungrily, or ravenously.
Devoted to religion or to religious feelings and duties; pious; extremely religious; godly.
Any moisture from the atmosphere condensed by cool bodies upon their surfaces.
Skill in performing tasks, especially with the hands.
A diminutive of the male given name Desmond.
A meaningless syllable used when singing a tune or indicating a rhythm.
A language spoken in Sandaun Province, Papua New Guinea.

Diabetes mellitus; any of a group of metabolic diseases whereby a person (or other animal) has high blood sugar due to an inability to produce, or inability to metabolize, sufficient quantities of the hormone insulin.
Of or pertaining to diabetes, especially diabetes mellitus.
Of, being, or pertaining to a devil or the Devil.
To determine which disease is causing a sick person's signs and symptoms; to find the diagnosis.
The process of, or an instance of, identification of the nature and cause of a medical condition or illness.
Joining two nonadjacent vertices (of a polygon or polyhedron).
A plan, drawing, sketch or outline to show the function or operation of something, or to show the relationships between the parts of a whole.
A graduated, circular scale over which a needle moves to show a measurement (such as speed).

A lect (often a regional or minority language) as part of a group or family of languages, especially if they are viewed as a single language, or if contrasted with a standardized idiom that is considered the 'true' form of the language (for example, Bavarian as contrasted with Standard German).
Any formal system of reasoning that arrives at a truth by the exchange of logical arguments.
A conversation or other form of discourse between two or more individuals.
Any straight line between two points on the circumference of a circle that passes through the centre/center of the circle; a chord that passes through the center of the circle.
A glimmering glass-like mineral that is an allotrope of carbon in which each atom is surrounded by four others in the form of a tetrahedron.

An absorbent garment worn around the crotch that retains the wearer's urine and feces, often worn by a baby or young child who is not yet toilet trained, or by an adult who is incontinent or wets the bed, or under extreme working conditions without access to a toilet; a nappy.
A gastrointestinal disorder characterized by frequent and very soft or watery bowel movements.
A daily log of experiences, especially those of the writer.
Gaming with one or more dice.
A separation or division into two; a distinction that results in such a division.
Dictating, the process of speaking for someone else to write down the words.
A totalitarian leader of a country, nation, or government.
A type of government where absolute sovereignty is allotted to an individual or a small clique.
Choice and use of words, especially with regard to effective communication.

A reference work listing words or names from one or more languages, usually ordered alphabetically, explaining each word's meanings or senses, oftentimes also containing information on its etymology, pronunciation, usage, semantic relations, translations, as well as other relevant information.
An authoritative statement; a dogmatic saying; a maxim, an apothegm.
To stop living; to become dead; to undergo death.
A village and commune of Arad County, Romania.

A fuel derived from petroleum (or other oils) but heavier than gasoline/petrol. Used to power diesel engines which burn this fuel using the heat produced when air is compressed.
The food and beverage a person or animal consumes.
A regulated diet that excludes or constrains certain types of food, especially for health reasons; also, a book or similar setting out such regulations.
Not to have the same traits or characteristics; to be unalike or distinct.
Not the same; exhibiting a difference.

Hard, not easy, requiring much effort.

To move hard-packed earth out of the way, especially downward to make a hole with a shovel. Or to drill, or the like, through rocks, roads, or the like. More generally, to make any similar hole by moving material out of the way.
To distribute or arrange methodically; to work over and classify; to reduce to portions for ready use or application.
The process, in the gastrointestinal tract, by which food is converted into substances that can be used by the body.

A position in a sequence of numerals representing a place value in a positional number system.
Having to do with digits (fingers or toes); especially, performed with a finger.
To invest with dignity or honour.
The state of being dignified or worthy of esteem: elevation of mind or character.

Defence Imagery and Geospatial Organisation, former name of AGO - an Australian government intelligence agency responsible for the tasking (collection), exploitation (analysis), and dissemination (distribution) of geospatial intelligence (GEOINT).

A circumstance in which a choice must be made between two or more alternatives that seem equally undesirable.
Steady application; industry; careful work involving long-term effort.
Performing with industrious concentration; hard-working and focused.
To make thinner by adding solvent to a solution, especially by adding water.
A coin worth one-tenth of a dollar, that is, ten cents.
A single aspect of a given thing.
To eat; to eat dinner or supper.
A small open boat, propelled by oars or paddles, carried as a tender, lifeboat, or pleasure craft on a ship.

Eating dinner as a social function.
The main meal of the day, often eaten in the evening.
Those animals of the clade Dinosauria that existed during the Triassic, Jurassic and Cretaceous periods and are now extinct; that is, any member of the clade Dinosauria other than birds.
Initialism of Defence Intelligence Organisation, an Australian government intelligence agency.
Abbreviation of distal intestinal obstruction syndrome.
Any oxide containing two oxygen atoms in each molecule.
A lower section of a road or geological feature.
A document issued by an educational institution testifying that the recipient has earned a degree or has successfully completed a particular course of study.

The art and practice of conducting international relations by negotiating alliances, treaties, agreements etc., bilaterally or multilaterally, between states and sometimes international organizations, or even between polities with varying status, such as those of monarchs and their princely vassals.
A person, such as an ambassador, who is accredited to represent a government officially in its relations with other governments or international organisations
Concerning the relationships between the governments of countries.
Warning of bad consequences: ill-boding; portentous.

A theoretical line (physically or mentally) followed from a point of origin or towards a destination. May be relative (e.g. up, left, outbound, dorsal), geographical (e.g. north), rotational (e.g. clockwise), or with respect to an object or location (e.g. toward Boston).
That which directs; serving to direct, indicate, or guide.
In a direct manner; in a straight line or course.
One who directs; the person in charge of managing a department or directorate (e.g., director of engineering), project, or production (as in a show or film, e.g., film director).
A list of names, addresses etc, of specific classes of people or organizations, often in alphabetical order or in some classification.
A Roman Catholic service for the dead, being the first antiphon of matins for the dead, of which dirige is the first word; a dirge.
Unclean; covered with or containing unpleasant substances such as dirt or grime.
A condition characterised by a significant limitation in a person's physical or intellectual powers or ability.

To condemn; to consider wrong or inappropriate; used with of.
To deprive of weapons; to deprive of the means of attack or defense; to render defenseless.
